chapter: JEF P106 – The Making of a Scientist
title: The Making of a Scientist – Easy SAQs

questions.question: Who is Richard Ebright?
questions.answer: Richard Ebright is a young scientist whose life story is described in the lesson.

questions.question: Name the author of ‘The Making of a Scientist’.
questions.answer: The author of the lesson is Robert W. Peterson.

questions.question: What hobby did Richard Ebright develop as a child?
questions.answer: Richard Ebright developed the hobby of collecting butterflies.

questions.question: How did Ebright’s mother support his learning?
questions.answer: She encouraged his curiosity and provided books and scientific equipment.

questions.question: Which book inspired Ebright to learn science seriously?
questions.answer: The book ‘The Travels of Monarch X’ inspired him.

questions.question: Why did Ebright lose his first science fair?
questions.answer: He lost because he did not perform a real scientific experiment.

questions.question: What lesson did Ebright learn from his failure?
questions.answer: He learned that real science requires experimentation.

questions.question: Which insect did Ebright mainly study?
questions.answer: Ebright mainly studied the monarch butterfly.

questions.question: Who guided Ebright by suggesting experiments?
questions.answer: Dr Frederick A. Urquhart guided Ebright by suggesting experiments.

questions.question: What discovery did Ebright make about monarch pupae?
questions.answer: He discovered that the gold spots produce hormones needed for growth.

questions.question: What quality drove Ebright towards science?
questions.answer: Curiosity drove Ebright towards science.

questions.question: What does the lesson say about intelligence?
questions.answer: Intelligence must be supported by curiosity and hard work.

questions.question: What is meant by scientific temper?
questions.answer: Scientific temper is a questioning and evidence-based attitude.

questions.question: How did failure help Ebright?
questions.answer: Failure motivated him to improve his scientific approach.

questions.question: What environment helped Ebright succeed?
questions.answer: A supportive family and academic environment helped him succeed.

questions.question: Which quality helped Ebright overcome challenges?
questions.answer: Perseverance helped Ebright overcome challenges.

questions.question: What kind of learning does the lesson promote?
questions.answer: The lesson promotes inquiry-based learning.

questions.question: Why is Richard Ebright an inspiration to students?
questions.answer: He turned curiosity and hard work into scientific achievement.

questions.question: What does the title ‘The Making of a Scientist’ suggest?
questions.answer: It suggests that a scientist is shaped through effort and learning.

questions.question: Which journal published Ebright’s scientific work?
questions.answer: His work was published in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Science.

questions.question: What role did experiments play in Ebright’s success?
questions.answer: Experiments helped him understand real science deeply.

questions.question: What message does the lesson convey to students?
questions.answer: Curiosity and perseverance lead to success.

questions.question: What distinguishes Ebright from rote learners?
questions.answer: His focus on experimentation distinguishes him from rote learners.

questions.question: What does Ebright’s journey teach about failure?
questions.answer: Failure is a learning opportunity, not an end.

questions.question: State the central theme of the lesson.
questions.answer: The central theme is the making of a scientist through curiosity and effort.
